case_title: FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA, A NATIONAL BANKING VS. ROBERT CHAN et al

docket_entries.description: COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY DEFENDANT CHAN, ROBERT AN INDIVIDUAL
DECLARATION OF SEVICE BY MAIL OF OPPOS. TO DEFT'S MTN TO DISMISS AND DEC. FILED BY PLAINTIFF FIRST NATIONAL BANK OF OMAHA, A NATIONAL BANKING ASSOCIATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN'S MOTION TO WITHDRAW PLEADING AND DISMISS CASE IS GRANTED, IN PART. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO WITHDRAW HIS GENERAL DENIAL FILED ON NOVEMBER 13, 2012 IS GRANTED. (CCP 473.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT OPPOSE THE WITHDRAWAL OF THIS PLEADING. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO BE DISMISSED FROM THE ACTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT PRESENTS NO LEGAL AUTHORITY TO SUPPORT DISMISSAL ON THE ASSERTED FACTUAL GROUNDS. " ' "[A] POINT WHICH IS MERELY SUGGESTED BY [A PARTY'S] COUNSEL, WITH NO SUPPORTING ARGUMENT OR AUTHORITY, IS DEEMED TO BE WITHOUT FOUNDATION AND REQUIRES NO DISCUSSION." [CITATION.]' [CITATION.]" (DO IT URSELF MOVING & STORAGE, INC. V. BROWN, LEIFER, SLATKIN & BERNS (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 27, 35, SUPERSEDED BY STATUTE ON OTHER GROUNDS IN UNION BANK V. SUP. CT. (1995) 31 CAL.APP.4TH 573, 582.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS CONTINUED FROM FEB-13-2015 TO MAR-11-2015, AT 9:30 AM, IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 13, 2015 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ARE SET ASIDE. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE ONLY WAY TO ENSURE THAT ROBERT KWOK CHU CHAN IS NOT ADVERSELY AFFECTED BY THE JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION IS TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT, PARTICULARLY IN LIGHT OF THE FACT THAT ROBERT KWOK CHU CHAN'S HOME ADDRESS IS LISTED ON THE OCTOBER 22, 2013 ENTRY OF DEFAULT AND THE DECEMBER 12, 2013 DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE COURT SIGNS DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER IN OPEN COURT, APPROVING THE LANGUAGE IN THE ORDER THAT DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE DAWN GHENO, CSR #7489; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Re Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Mar-25-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JAMES MACLEOD of the Dunning Law Firm, Tel No. (858) 974-7600 appearing telephonically for Plaintiff First National Bank of Omaha;
Atty. HANLEN J. CHANG, Tel No. (415) 757-3422 appearing for Defendant Robert Chan.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN'S Motion To Set Aside Default is granted. The default and default judgment are set aside. The Court finds that the only way to ensure that Robert Kwok Chu Chan is not adversely affected by the judgment in this action is to set aside the default and default judgment, particularly in light of the fact that Robert Kwok Chu Chan's home address is listed on the October 22, 2013 Entry of Default and the December 12, 2013 Default Judgment. The Court signs Defendant's proposed order in open court, approving the language in the order that Defendant Robert Chan is dismissed with prejudice. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Re Motion To Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Feb-13-2015 Continued To Mar-11-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN'S Motion To Set Aside Default is continued to March 11, 2015 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 13, 2015 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The Court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion To Withdraw Pleading And Dismiss Case
Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT ROBERT CHAN's Motion To Withdraw Pleading And Dismiss Case is Granted, in part. Defendant's request to withdraw his general denial filed on November 13, 2012 is granted. (CCP 473.) Plaintiff does not oppose the withdrawal of this pleading. Defendant's request to be dismissed from the action is denied without prejudice. Defendant presents no legal authority to support dismissal on the asserted factual grounds. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
